highlights.text: I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
Elevator down refers to a period where ES sells rapidly, ignoring supports in the process.
Every point of elevator down sell is mostly paid back to bulls in the form of an equally or more violent short squeeze.
They occur when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
From there ES went elevator down ultimately selling down to 6950 by 3:30AM Monday morning.
Every elevator down sell in ES sees a short squeeze.
We set an enormous low at 6955 this morning (from which we rallied to 7k+) and the Failed Breakdown of this low is an obvious long entry.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Are Bulls Running Out of Steam In SPX? January 14 Plan
